title: Tangy Crumbly Indian Skirt Steak Cumble
description: This monsoon-inspired dish features a tangy crumbly filling made from skirt steak, perfect for pescatarian meals and baked in the oven.
instructions: Chop the onion and garlic into small pieces.
Heat oil in a pan, add onions and sauté until translucent.
Add minced garlic and cook for another minute.
Mix tomato paste with corn starch or arrowroot powder to form a smooth sauce.
Pour this mixture over the onions and garlic, stir well until thickened.
Add cumin seeds, crushed cardamom pods, garam masala, red chilli paste, tamarind paste, coconut milk (or almond milk), and 1 cup of water. Stir to combine.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for about 30 minutes until the sauce thickens.
Cut skirt steak into small cubes, approximately 1 inch in size.
Toss the cubed meat in cornstarch or arrowroot powder before adding them to the sauce.
Simmer the meat in the sauce mixture for 5-7 minutes until heated through.
Sprinkle sesame seeds over the top of the creme just before serving.
Serve warm as a snack with some vegetables or whole wheat bread.
